title: Te Whare Tū Taua o Kahungunu: Building a Traditional Pātaka (Food Storage House) / The Warrior House of Kahungunu
subject: Technology
culturalContext: Students will explore traditional Ngāti Kahungunu construction methods by designing and building scale models of pātaka (elevated food storage houses), connecting to the historical pātaka that once stood along the Mangakōtukutuku Stream to preserve kai moana and seasonal harvests for the hapū.
learningObjectives: Design and construct a functional scale model pātaka using traditional Māori joinery techniques (tāwhiti, whakakopa)
Analyze how pātaka design reflects Ngāti Kahungunu environmental knowledge of flood plains and seasonal storage needs
Create a materials list specifying both traditional (harakeke, tōtara) and modern sustainable alternatives
Present design choices explaining how they honor both traditional wisdom and modern sustainability principles

activities.title: Whakapapa of Wood
activities.description: Visit local stands of tōtara and learn from kaumātua about traditional wood selection, harvesting protocols (rahui), and the significance of different native timbers in Ngāti Kahungunu construction

activities.title: Joining the Ancestors
activities.description: Hands-on workshop practicing traditional joinery techniques using replica tools, focusing on interlocking joints that require no nails or modern fasteners

activities.title: Pātaka Design Wānanga
activities.description: Design studio session where students create scaled blueprints for their pātaka, incorporating ventilation systems, elevation considerations, and materials planning based on local resources

activities.title: Model Construction
activities.description: Build physical scale models using both traditional materials (harakeke cordage, wood) and modern sustainable alternatives, testing structural integrity and functionality

activities.title: Whakamārama Presentation
activities.description: Students present their completed pātaka models to community experts, explaining design choices and how they blend traditional knowledge with contemporary sustainable practices

resources: Ngāti Kahungunu kaumātua Hōri Reti (local traditional building expert)
Te Ara Encyclopedia entry on Traditional Māori Building Techniques
Hawke's Bay Regional Council flood plain maps and historical climate data
Sustainable Timber Institute's guide to native wood alternatives

tasks: Completed scale model pātaka demonstrating traditional joinery techniques
Design portfolio showing blueprint, materials list, and environmental considerations
5-minute oral presentation explaining cultural and technical design choices

nzcAlignment: Technological Practice (Level 4): Planning for practice, brief development, outcome development and evaluation
Nature of Technology (Level 4): Characteristics of technological outcomes, characteristics of technology
Technology and Society: Understand how technology and society influence each other
